articles.title: Update of guideline for diagnosis and treatment of subacromial pain syndrome: a multidisciplinary review by the Dutch Orthopedic Association Part 2: Operative considerations and treatment of various conditions related to subacromial pain syndrome.
articles.authors: Lambers Heerspink FO, Veen EJD, Dorrestijn O, Visser CPJ, Leijs MJC, Van Poppel D, et al.
articles.population: Patients with subacromial pain syndrome, including supraspinatus tears, biceps tendon pathology, and calcific tendinosis
articles.outcomes: Recommendations for operative management of supraspinatus tears, biceps tendon pathology, and calcific tendinosis
articles.take_home: Exercise-based therapy with corticosteroid injection should be the initial treatment for isolated symptomatic non-traumatic supraspinatus tears, with cuff repair considered if no improvement after 3-6 months. Barbotage is recommended for calcific tendinosis with surgery reserved for persistent large calcifications.
articles.relevance_reason: Guidelines on surgical treatment of supraspinatus tears and rotator cuff tendinopathy

articles.title: Update of guideline for diagnosis and treatment of subacromial pain syndrome: a multidisciplinary review by the Dutch Orthopedic Association Part 1: preventive measures, diagnostics, and non-surgical treatment of subacromial pain syndrome.
articles.authors: Lambers Heerspink FO, Veen EJD, Dorrestijn O, Visser CPJ, Leijs MJC, Van Poppel D, et al.
articles.population: Patients with subacromial pain syndrome (SAPS): non-traumatic, non-rheumatologic shoulder complaints painful during arm elevation
articles.outcomes: Recommendations for prevention, diagnosis, imaging, and non-surgical treatment of SAPS
articles.take_home: The updated Dutch guideline recommends using a cluster of physical diagnostic tests for diagnosis, ultrasonography for suspected supraspinatus rupture, and conservative management including exercise therapy, corticosteroid injections, and suprascapular nerve block for therapy-resistant cases. Patient education about healthy lifestyle and gradual exercise is emphasised for prevention.
articles.relevance_reason: Guidelines on non-surgical treatment and diagnosis of rotator cuff tendinopathy

articles.title: Risk factors affecting rotator cuff retear after arthroscopic repair: a meta-analysis and systematic review.
articles.authors: Zhao J, Luo M, Pan J, Liang G, Feng W, Zeng L, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing arthroscopic rotator cuff repair
articles.outcomes: Risk factors for rotator cuff retear after arthroscopic repair
articles.take_home: Multiple risk factors for rotator cuff retear were identified including patient factors (age, BMI, diabetes), imaging findings (fatty infiltration, tear size, critical shoulder angle), and surgical factors (operative duration, biceps procedure). These findings can help identify high-risk patients early and guide targeted prevention strategies.
articles.relevance_reason: Systematic review of risk factors for retear after arthroscopic rotator cuff repair

articles.title: Bioinductive patch as an augmentation for rotator cuff repair, a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Warren JR, Domingo-Johnson ER, Sorensen AA, Cheng AL, Latz KH, Cil A
articles.population: Patients with full and partial-thickness rotator cuff tears undergoing repair
articles.outcomes: American Shoulder and Elbow Surgeon score, Constant-Murley Score, visual analog scale for pain, minimal clinically important difference, tendon thickness, retear rate, and complication rate
articles.take_home: Bioinductive collagen patch augmentation for rotator cuff repair resulted in an 8.3% retear rate for full-thickness tears and 1.1% for partial-thickness tears, with similar patient-reported outcome improvements to standard repair and lower overall retear rates compared to traditional repair, though complication rates were similar for full-thickness and higher for partial-thickness tears. The patch appears safe with only 0.2% adverse reactions.
articles.relevance_reason: Meta-analysis of surgical augmentation technique for rotator cuff repair outcomes

articles.title: Common elective orthopaedic procedures and their clinical effectiveness: umbrella review of level 1 evidence.
articles.authors: Blom AW, Donovan RL, Beswick AD, Whitehouse MR, Kunutsor SK
articles.population: Patients undergoing 10 common elective orthopaedic procedures (ACL reconstruction, meniscal repair, meniscectomy, rotator cuff repair, subacromial decompression, carpal tunnel decompression, lumbar spine decompression, lumbar spine fusion, total hip replacement, total knee replacement)
articles.outcomes: Clinical effectiveness of orthopaedic procedures compared with no treatment, placebo, or non-operative care
articles.take_home: Randomised controlled trial evidence supports only carpal tunnel decompression and total knee replacement over non-operative care. Trial evidence for six other procedures showed no benefit over non-operative care, despite these procedures being recommended in national guidelines.
articles.relevance_reason: Includes arthroscopic rotator cuff repair and subacromial decompression effectiveness

articles.title: Treatment of rotator cuff tears: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Lapner P, Henry P, Athwal GS, Moktar J, McNeil D, MacDonald P
articles.population: Patients aged ≥18 years with full-thickness rotator cuff tears
articles.sample_size: 1096 randomized patients in SR vs. DR fixation meta-analysis
articles.outcomes: Patient-reported function, pain, and reoperation/retear rates
articles.take_home: Double-row fixation achieves similar functional and pain outcomes compared to single-row fixation but results in lower retear rates. Latissimus dorsi transfer produces comparable functional outcomes to partial repair, lower trapezius transfer, and superior capsular reconstruction, though high-quality comparative trials are needed.
articles.relevance_reason: Systematic review comparing surgical treatment options for full-thickness rotator cuff tears

articles.title: Operative versus nonoperative treatment for the management of full-thickness rotator cuff tears: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Piper CC, Hughes AJ, Ma Y, Wang H, Neviaser AS
articles.population: Patients with full-thickness rotator cuff tears
articles.outcomes: Constant score and visual analog scale (VAS) pain score at 1 year
articles.take_home: Operative treatment showed statistically significant improvements in Constant and VAS scores compared to nonoperative treatment at 1 year, but the differences were small and did not reach clinical significance. Larger studies with longer follow-up are needed to determine if clinically meaningful differences emerge over time.
articles.relevance_reason: Systematic review comparing operative versus nonoperative treatment of full-thickness rotator cuff tears

articles.title: Mid- to long-term outcomes of latissimus dorsi tendon transfer for massive irreparable posterosuperior rotator cuff tears: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Velasquez Garcia A, Nieboer MJ, de Marinis R, Morrey ME, Valenti P, Sanchez-Sotelo J
articles.population: Patients with massive irreparable posterosuperior rotator cuff tears treated with latissimus dorsi tendon transfer
articles.outcomes: Constant-Murley Score, visual analog scale pain, forward flexion, abduction, external rotation, complication rate, and revision surgery rate
articles.take_home: Latissimus dorsi tendon transfer significantly improves functional outcomes, pain relief, and range of motion in patients with massive irreparable posterosuperior rotator cuff tears at mid-to long-term follow-up, with complication and revision rates of 13% and 6% respectively. In well-selected patients, this procedure provides favourable outcomes for this challenging problem.
articles.relevance_reason: Systematic review of latissimus dorsi transfer outcomes for massive irreparable rotator cuff tears

articles.title: The clinical impact of retears after repair of posterosuperior rotator cuff tears: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Holtedahl R, Boe B, Brox JI
articles.population: Patients undergoing surgical repair of full-thickness posterosuperior rotator cuff tears
articles.outcomes: Postoperative cuff integrity, shoulder pain, function (Constant-Murley score, American Shoulder and Elbow Surgeons score), muscle strength, and health-related quality of life
articles.take_home: Retears after rotator cuff repair were associated with statistically significant but clinically modest reductions in pain and function scores, with differences below minimal clinically important thresholds. Most patients achieved satisfactory outcomes despite postoperative retears.
articles.relevance_reason: Systematic review analyzing clinical impact of retears after posterosuperior rotator cuff repair

articles.title: The effects of smoking on clinical and structural outcomes after rotator cuff repair: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Fan N, Yuan S, Du P, Wu Q, Li J, Kong X, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing arthroscopic and open rotator cuff repair
articles.outcomes: American Shoulder and Elbow Surgeons score, Simple Shoulder Test score, University of California-Los Angeles score, visual analog scale score, Constant score, rotator cuff retear rate, reoperation rate
articles.take_home: Smoking was associated with a significantly increased risk of retear (2.06 times higher) and reoperation (1.29 times higher) after rotator cuff repair, though most clinical outcome scores were similar between smokers and nonsmokers except for a significantly lower Constant score in smokers.
articles.relevance_reason: Systematic review of smoking effects on outcomes after rotator cuff repair

articles.title: Comparison of the effects of platelet-rich plasma and corticosteroid injection in rotator cuff disease treatment: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Peng Y, Li F, Ding Y, Sun X, Wang G, Jia S, et al.
articles.population: Patients with rotator cuff disease
articles.outcomes: Clinical function (Constant score, Simple Shoulder Test, American Shoulder and Elbow Surgeons score) and pain (visual analog scale) at short-term, midterm, and long-term follow-up
articles.take_home: Corticosteroid injection provides superior short-term pain relief and functional improvement compared to platelet-rich plasma, whilst platelet-rich plasma demonstrates better long-term functional recovery. No clinically meaningful differences were observed between treatments.
articles.relevance_reason: Systematic review comparing PRP and corticosteroid injection for rotator cuff disease treatment

articles.title: The timing of retears after arthroscopic rotator cuff repair.
articles.authors: Chona DV, Lakomkin N, Lott A, Workman AD, Henry AC, Kuntz AF, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing arthroscopic rotator cuff repair
articles.outcomes: Retear rates at different time points stratified by tear size (medium, large, massive)
articles.take_home: Retear rates increase over the first 10-15 months after arthroscopic rotator cuff repair, then stabilise, with rates varying by tear size (approximately 20% for medium tears, 40% for large tears, 20-60% for massive tears). Retear timing and progression depend on the size of the original tear.
articles.relevance_reason: Examines retear timing and failure rates after arthroscopic rotator cuff repair surgery

articles.title: Tranexamic acid use in arthroscopic rotator cuff repair is an effective and safe adjunct to improve visualization: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Han C, Liu M, Lian X, Sun T, Yan S, Bai X, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing arthroscopic rotator cuff repair, with or without tranexamic acid
articles.outcomes: Primary: visual clarity. Secondary: operative time, postoperative pain, blood loss, shoulder swelling, irrigation fluid volume, pump pressure adjustments, adverse cardiovascular events
articles.take_home: Tranexamic acid significantly improves arthroscopic visual clarity and reduces operative time in arthroscopic rotator cuff repair without increasing adverse events. The optimal dosing and administration route remain to be established.
articles.relevance_reason: Directly addresses adjunct intervention in arthroscopic rotator cuff repair with clinical outcomes

articles.title: Chronic calcific tendonitis of the rotator cuff: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials comparing operative and nonoperative interventions.
articles.authors: Angileri HS, Gohal C, Comeau-Gauthier M, Owen MM, Shanmugaraj A, Terry MA, et al.
articles.population: Patients with chronic calcific tendonitis of the rotator cuff
articles.outcomes: Pain on visual analog scale (VAS), Constant-Murley Shoulder Outcome Score (CMS), and resolution of calcific deposits
articles.take_home: Surgical treatment produces larger improvements in functional outcome scores (CMS) compared to nonoperative interventions including extracorporeal shockwave therapy and ultrasonography-guided needling, with comparable pain reduction. Both operative and nonoperative treatments result in clinically significant improvements, supporting a trial of ultrasonography-guided needling and extracorporeal shockwave therapy as first-line treatment.
articles.relevance_reason: Compares operative and nonoperative treatments for calcific rotator cuff tendinitis with clinical outcomes

articles.title: Factors associated with reparability of rotator cuff tears: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Hsu KL, Kuan FC, Velasquez Garcia A, Hong CK, Chen Y, Shih CA, et al.
articles.population: Patients with rotator cuff tears
articles.outcomes: Factors associated with reparability of rotator cuff tears, including age, tear size, fatty infiltration, muscle atrophy, and humeral head migration
articles.take_home: Older age, larger tear size, severe fatty infiltration, muscle atrophy, and advanced superior migration of the humeral head are strongly associated with irreparable rotator cuff tears. The tangent sign is a powerful and simple tool for predicting reparability.
articles.relevance_reason: Identifies factors associated with rotator cuff tear reparability for surgical decision-making

articles.title: Anatomic shoulder parameters and their relationship to the presence of degenerative rotator cuff tears and glenohumeral osteoarthritis: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Zaid MB, Young NM, Pedoia V, Feeley BT, Ma CB, Lansdown DA
articles.population: Patients with degenerative rotator cuff tears and glenohumeral osteoarthritis
articles.outcomes: Relationship between scapular anatomic parameters (acromial index, critical shoulder angle, lateral acromial angle, glenoid inclination) and degenerative rotator cuff tears and glenohumeral osteoarthritis
articles.take_home: Increased critical shoulder angle and acromial index correlated with degenerative rotator cuff tears, whilst decreased lateral acromial angle also associated with rotator cuff tears. No significant relationship was found between these scapular parameters and glenohumeral osteoarthritis.
articles.relevance_reason: Systematic review of anatomic factors associated with degenerative rotator cuff tears

articles.title: Rotator cuff repair vs. nonoperative treatment: a systematic review with meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Brindisino F, Salomon M, Giagio S, Pastore C, Innocenti T
articles.population: Patients with rotator cuff tears of any type
articles.outcomes: Function and pain perception at 6, 12, and 24 months
articles.take_home: Rotator cuff repair showed statistically significant improvements in pain and function compared with conservative treatment at 6, 12, and 24 months, but these differences did not reach minimum clinically important thresholds. The certainty of evidence was low to moderate due to imprecision.
articles.relevance_reason: Directly compares surgical repair to nonoperative treatment for rotator cuff tears with clinical outcomes

articles.title: Outcomes of subacromial balloon spacer implantation for irreparable rotator cuff tears: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Berk AN, Cregar WM, Gachigi KK, Trofa DP, Schiffern SC, Hamid N, et al.
articles.population: Patients with irreparable rotator cuff tears treated with subacromial balloon spacer implantation
articles.sample_size: 886 patients (894 shoulders) across 17 studies included in meta-analysis
articles.outcomes: Constant score, American Shoulder and Elbow Surgeons score, Oxford Shoulder Score, pain scores, range of motion (forward elevation, abduction, external rotation), device-related complications, and need for salvage reverse shoulder arthroplasty
articles.take_home: Subacromial balloon spacer implantation resulted in significant improvements in pain, function and range of motion at short-term follow-up, with low device-related complication rates (3.6%) and 5% requiring salvage reverse shoulder arthroplasty. However, long-term outcomes and comparative effectiveness relative to other treatment strategies remain unknown.
articles.relevance_reason: Evaluates surgical outcomes of balloon spacer for irreparable rotator cuff tears

articles.title: The clinical efficacy of leukocyte-poor platelet-rich plasma in arthroscopic rotator cuff repair: a meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
articles.authors: Zhao D, Han YH, Pan JK, Yang WY, Zeng LF, Liang GH, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing arthroscopic rotator cuff repair
articles.outcomes: Postoperative pain (visual analog scale), shoulder function (Constant and UCLA scores), retear rates
articles.take_home: Leukocyte-poor PRP significantly reduces retear rates in medium and long-term follow-up after arthroscopic rotator cuff repair, regardless of tear size or repair method. However, PRP provides only statistically significant but not clinically meaningful improvements in pain relief and functional outcomes.
articles.relevance_reason: Meta-analysis of PRP in arthroscopic rotator cuff repair outcomes

articles.title: Does early motion lead to a higher failure rate or better outcomes after arthroscopic rotator cuff repair? A systematic review of overlapping meta-analyses.
articles.authors: Saltzman BM, Zuke WA, Go B, Mascarenhas R, Verma NN, Cole BJ, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing arthroscopic rotator cuff repair comparing early-motion versus delayed-motion rehabilitation protocols
articles.outcomes: Clinical outcome scores, tendon healing, rotator cuff tear recurrence rates, range of motion, functional improvements, strength
articles.take_home: Early-motion rehabilitation after rotator cuff repair produces superior range of motion up to one year postoperatively compared to delayed motion, but both approaches result in comparable functional outcomes and retear rates overall. Early motion may result in greater retear rates particularly with larger preoperative tear sizes.
articles.relevance_reason: Systematic review of rehabilitation protocols after arthroscopic rotator cuff repair

articles.title: The effect of perioperative platelet-rich plasma injections on postoperative failure rates following rotator cuff repair: a systematic review with meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Cavendish PA, Everhart JS, DiBartola AC, Eikenberry AD, Cvetanovich GL, Flanigan DC
articles.population: Patients undergoing rotator cuff repair with or without perioperative platelet-rich plasma injection
articles.outcomes: Postoperative failure rates defined as subsequent tear on postoperative imaging
articles.take_home: Perioperative PRP injection resulted in a 25% reduction in the risk of rotator cuff repair failure with low heterogeneity among studies, demonstrating a consistent protective effect regardless of tear size. However, the variability in PRP preparations prevents a specific clinical recommendation.
articles.relevance_reason: Meta-analysis of PRP on failure rates after rotator cuff repair

articles.title: Association of obesity with high retears and complication rates, and low functional scores after rotator cuff repair: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Yang Z, Chen W, Liang J, Liu T, Zhang B, Wang X, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing rotator cuff repair, stratified by obesity status
articles.outcomes: Retear rates, reoperation rates, complication rates, American Shoulder and Elbow Surgeons scores, visual analog scale for pain, surgical duration, and external rotation
articles.take_home: Obese patients undergoing rotator cuff repair have significantly higher retear rates, reoperation rates, and complication rates, along with lower functional scores and greater postoperative pain compared to non-obese patients. Obesity did not affect surgical duration or external rotation outcomes.
articles.relevance_reason: Meta-analysis of obesity effect on rotator cuff repair outcomes including retear rates

articles.title: Surgical repair versus conservative treatment and subacromial decompression for the treatment of rotator cuff tears: a meta-analysis of randomized trials.
articles.authors: Schemitsch C, Chahal J, Vicente M, Nowak L, Flurin PH, Lambers Heerspink F, et al.
articles.population: Patients with chronic/degenerative rotator cuff tears
articles.outcomes: Constant-Murley Score at one year; crossover rate to surgical repair in conservative treatment group
articles.take_home: Surgical repair resulted in statistically significantly better Constant-Murley scores at one year compared with conservative treatment and subacromial decompression alone, although the magnitude of difference may be small and conservative treatment success rates were relatively high. Eleven point nine per cent of conservatively treated patients eventually underwent surgical repair.
articles.relevance_reason: Meta-analysis comparing surgical repair versus conservative treatment for rotator cuff tears

articles.title: Obesity and sex influence fatty infiltration of the rotator cuff: the Rotator Cuff Outcomes Workgroup (ROW) and Multicenter Orthopaedic Outcomes Network (MOON) cohorts.
articles.authors: Giri A, Freeman TH, Kim P, Kuhn JE, Garriga GA, Khazzam M, et al.
articles.population: Patients with rotator cuff tears from two multicenter cohorts (MOON and ROW)
articles.outcomes: Fatty infiltration of the rotator cuff in relation to BMI and sex
articles.take_home: Obesity and female sex are significantly associated with fatty infiltration in rotator cuff tears. Obese patients had 3.28-fold increased odds of fatty infiltration compared to normal BMI, and women were 4.9 times more likely to have fatty infiltration than men.
articles.relevance_reason: Studies relationship between obesity and fatty infiltration as prognostic factor for rotator cuff surgery outcomes

articles.title: Bone channeling in arthroscopic rotator cuff repair: a systematic review and meta-analysis of level I studies.
articles.authors: Fairley JA, Pollock JW, McIlquham K, Lapner P
articles.population: Patients aged ≥18 years with full-thickness rotator cuff tears undergoing arthroscopic repair
articles.outcomes: Functional scores (Constant-Murley), retear rates, pain, and reoperation rates
articles.take_home: Bone marrow channeling added to arthroscopic rotator cuff repair provides no significant benefit for functional outcomes, healing rates, pain reduction, or reoperation rates compared to repair alone. Retear rates were similar between groups at approximately 20% in both channeling and control arms.
articles.relevance_reason: Systematic review and meta-analysis of bone channeling technique in arthroscopic rotator cuff repair
